title: Claude Code Token Optimization — 3 Techniques
desc: 80-90% of tokens are spent on file exploration, not writing code. Three techniques to cut waste: (1) .claudeignore + lean CLAUDE.md + session management saves 30-40%. (2) Codebase pre-indexing saves 90%+ of exploration tokens. (3) 3-agent team (Architect/Builder/Reviewer) eliminates scope creep token waste.
steps: [CLAUDEIGNORE] Create .claudeignore in project root. Exclude: node_modules/, dist/, bin/, obj/, .bak/, .log, .out, .wkappbot/, .dotnet/. One-time setup, no maintenance needed.
[CLAUDE-MD] Keep CLAUDE.md under 200 lines. Every conversation loads it in full. Move detailed rules to separate topic files, reference them only when needed. Saves 5-10% per conversation.
[SESSION] Use /compact when conversation grows long. Use /clear when switching to a completely new task. Check context with wkappbot claude-usage -- handoff at 8MB, urgent at 10MB.
[MCP] Only connect MCP servers you actively use. Each connected server adds tool definitions to every message. Disconnecting 3 unused servers gives immediate relief.

[PRE-INDEX] Pre-index codebase with qmd (BM25+vector). Without indexing: glob+grep+read = 2700 tokens per lookup. With indexing: 250 tokens. 91% reduction. qmd://collection/file.cs:42 resolves directly to file+line.

[QMD-MODES] Three search modes: (1) qmd search = BM25 keyword/identifier, fast, no extra models. (2) qmd vsearch = vector semantic, concept queries, 329MB model. (3) qmd query = hybrid + LLM expansion + reranking, downloads 2GB models on first run. Use search for identifiers, vsearch for concepts, avoid query in daily use.

[QMD-TIPS] Search without --collection searches all collections at once (better than filtering). Exact function names return precise file:line. Natural language works best with vsearch. MCP: add qmd server to .mcp.json, available as mcp__qmd__* tools.

[3-AGENT] Split work into 3 roles: (1) Architect reads codebase, writes detailed brief. (2) Builder implements ONLY what brief specifies. (3) Reviewer validates output, returns specific failures to Builder.
[3-AGENT-WHY] Without role separation, Claude reads the entire codebase for a login feature, adds untested CSS, writes unrequested tests. Builder never reads files the Architect did not specify.

[CLAUDE-MD-RULES] 5 rules to add: (1) Never re-read a file already in context. (2) No speculative tool calls. (3) Parallelize independent tool calls. (4) Never repeat what the user already explained. (5) Use qmd search before reading files.
[PRIORITY] Apply in order: first .claudeignore + lean CLAUDE.md (immediate, zero cost). Then qmd pre-indexing (5 min setup, 91% exploration savings). Then 3-agent structure when project grows large.
[IMPACT] Combined: same token budget handles 2-3x more work. For Claude Pro users with tight limits, this effectively doubles monthly task capacity.
author: claude
